fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

Patient had recent left hammertoe surgery on the left foot with podiatry.
Patient was told to be non-weight bearing primarily on the left foot.
He sat down and then when trying to get up and not put weight on his left foot, the patient fell onto his right hip.
Patient suffered a right proximal f emur fracture.
Patient was admitted to Honor Health Deer Valley.
Patient's creatinine was a little bit elevated.
Patient does have history of prior prostate procedure.
Patient had some retention but then was able to urinate.
Patient was recommended outpatient cysto and follow-up.
Patient had some postoperative anemia, did require a unit of bl ood postoperatively.
Patient also had some electrolyte inbal ances, hypokal hypomagnesem a that was repleted.

HI STORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: A 79-year-old male.
He was seen at the rehab.
He underwent right hip intramedullary nailing by Dr.
Wong on 03/08.
Workup showed that he had an intraluminal | adder mass, likely arising fromthe prostate and on CT on 03/24.
He was seen by urology.
They had difficulty passing a catheter.
Creatinine improved to 1.1.
He was recommended outpatient follow with bone and health clinic for his osteoporotic type fractures.
PAST MEDI CAL HI STORY: BPH, history of prior procedure on his prostate, history of sciatica.
FAMILY HI STORY: Noncontributory to the current presentation.
REVI EW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point systems reviewed and negative except as detailed in HPI.
GENERAL: This is a well-nourished, well-developed male, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Pupils equal, round, reactive tolight and accompdation.
Extraocular movements intact.
Micous membranes are pink and moist.
NECK: Supple, no JVD or thyromegaly.
HEART: Normal S1, S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
ABDOMEN: Soft, nontender, with no visceromegaly.
EXTREM TI ES: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema.
ASSESSMENT AND PLAN: 1.
Right f emur fracture, status post ORI F.
PMT\R has been consulted.
Postoperative anemia. we will follow hemoglobin. 3.
Foley not able to be replaced.
Creatinine was stable, 1-1.1 range.
No need outpatient monitoring of this.
He on Flomax.
This patient will be followed by his primary care provider who will be responsible for further review of the plan of care and recertification as applicable.

sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is confined to the home due to a right hip fracture, fall, and left foot surgery. The patient requires assistive devices such as a walker and assistance to leave home. Pain also interferes with outside mobility.
supportingSections: Home Health Certification and Face to Face Encounter Addendum

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient requires home health services due to a right hip fracture, fall, and left foot surgery. The patient is unable to ambulate without assistance and requires skilled nursing, occupational therapy, and physical therapy.
supportingSections: Home Health Certification and Face to Face Encounter Addendum

sourceHighlights.title: Prior level of function
sourceHighlights.summary: Prior to the recent injuries, the patient was able to ambulate with a knee scooter and use the toilet independently.

sourceHighlights.title: Wound history
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has a surgical dressing over the right anterior hip and a cam-type boot on the left foot and ankle.

sourceHighlights.title: Diet and fluid instructions
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is to be non-weight bearing on the left foot to allow for healing after left hammertoe surgery.

sourceHighlights.title: PMH, immunizations, diabetes
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has a history of BPH, prior prostate procedure, and sciatica. There are no known drug allergies. The patient is a nonsmoker and denies alcohol or illicit drug use.
supportingSections: Past Medical History, Allergies, Social History

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has diagnoses of right femur fracture, postoperative anemia, enlarged prostate with urinary retention, and a small left pleural effusion.

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is confined to the home due to a right hip fracture, fall, and left foot surgery. The patient requires assistive devices such as a walker and assistance to leave home. Pain also interferes with outside mobility.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires home health services due to a right hip fracture, fall, and left foot surgery. The patient is unable to ambulate without assistance and requires skilled nursing, occupational therapy, and physical therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: John Hansen, a 79-year-old male, was admitted with a right proximal femur fracture following a fall. He underwent right hip intramedullary nailing. The patient has a history of BPH, prior prostate procedure, and sciatica. Postoperative complications included anemia, urinary retention, and a small left pleural effusion. The patient requires home health services for skilled nursing, occupational therapy, and physical therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Skilled interventions include medication management, pain assessment and management, disease process education, gait training, ADL training, fall risk/balance assessment and training, and wound care.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: The next visit will focus on monitoring the patient's pain levels, wound healing, and progress with physical therapy and occupational therapy. Follow-up with urology and bone health clinic is also recommended.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problems: Right femur fracture, postoperative anemia, enlarged prostate with urinary retention, small left pleural effusion. Goals: Pain management, wound healing, improved mobility, and safe discharge planning. Interventions: Skilled nursing, occupational therapy, physical therapy, and medication management.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: The patient and caregiver goals include managing pain effectively, ensuring proper wound care, achieving improved mobility with the help of physical and occupational therapy, and preparing for a safe discharge from home health care.
